# Rothman, D. (2024) Transformers for Natural Language Processing and Computer Vision, Packt, Third Edition (Chapter 7) – available via Kortext

There is no logic for why GPT is decoder only or why bert is encoder only. It's mainly empirical.

GPT

- Task generalisation. You start a sentence and it can finish, which covers a variety of tasks.

An invention only becomes an innovation when it has an impact on society.

GPT might be considered as self-supervised rather than completely unsupervised. Since it predicts words

GPT relies on raw data, rather than labelled data. It's expensive and time consuming to label data.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Temperature will mean it doesn't choose the top choice, a high value like 0.9 means it takes more risk but could be more creative

Top_p, get the top n where the probability equalts top_p, then randonly chooses from them

Frequency penalty limits the frequency of a token in a given response

Presence_panlty forces the system to use new token and produce new ideas

## Rag

Improvement that compensates for lack of precision, training data and responses of GenAI. It can also avoid fine-tuning models in some cases.

In cursor with a venv, might need to restart go out and back in to load the latest version

False

False

False

False

False

False

False

True

# Main

## The platform business model

The platform business model is customer-to-customer(C2C).

Encourages sharing

Justpark - matching drivers to parking spaces

Zipcar - allows short-term car rentals

Blockchain would be a technology

Platform is a business model

Blockchain can facilitate or replace platform business.

Blockchain Examples

1. Arcase city - ride hailing app connecting drivers with customers
2. Winding Tree - travel marketplace connecting hotels, airlines and tourism offices
3. WAZ Marketplace - gamers buy and sell virtual accessories.

Crowdsourcing

1. Ideas - macdonals/lego use this to generate ideas
2. Skills and services - 99Design, uTest, Amazon Mechanical Turk.

## The experience economy

Virtual reality(VR), Extended reality(XR) and augmented reality(AR).

Retail include farfetch and browns to create interactive retail space.

Formulas E Ghost Racing allows you to race against real drivers

Disney magic bands

Immersive education and training

Warby Parker's AR tool for trying on glasses at home

Suits with haptic feedback, make things more realistic.

### Jourenal

Rayband glasses?

## Suprahuman Transformers

GPT-3 is built on GPT-2 architecture.

Fully trained GPT-3 is a foundation model, hence it can do many tasks it was not trained for.

It takes skill and knowledge to use GPT APIs. You need to understand the working of the foundation model to use it optimally.

Models went from training to fine-tuning and finally zero-shot in less than 3 years from 2017-2020.

Generative pre-training model(GPT) the perfection of fine-tuning with BERT.

![Timeline of the rise of billion-parameter transformer models, as described in the preceding text.](data:image/png;base64,)

Models evolved so fast they surpassed human baselines.

The number of parameters increased from 65M to 175B in GPT3

Parameters are the values in the internal model vectors and matrices generated by the word embeddings and attention calculations.

The number of parameters increased, but certain GPT3 models have less than GPT2

Number of layers increased from 6 layers to 96 layers in GPT3

The number of heads of a layer went from 8 to 96 in GPT3

The context size went from 512 token to 12,228 in GPT3

OpenAI train transformers to learn language

- Start with unsupervised training, then fine-tune via supervised learning
- Use decoder-only transformers (Note BERT is encoder only)
- Using metamodels which can be expressed as a probability distribution: p(output/input)

The goal was to generalise the concept to any type of downstream task once the trained GPT model understand a language through intensive training.

As parameters grew the results improved and fine-tuning was reduced.

Phrases that lead to zero-shot models

1. Fine-tuning
2. Few-Shot(FS) - Model is trained, then shown demonstrations of the task to complete. This is called conditioning and replaces weight updating.
3. One-shot(1S) - Only one demonstration is presented
4. Zero-shot(ZS) - No demonstration is given to the model of the task to be performed.

![Diagram of GPT model architecture, as described in the preceding text.](data:image/png;base64,)

Various engines

Base

- Davinci engine can analyse complex intent
- Curie engine is fast and has good summarisation
- Babbage engine is good at semantic search.
- Ada engine is good at parsing text.

More

- Instruct series provides instructions based on a description.
- Codex series can translate language to code.
- Content Filter series filters unsafe or sensitive text.

### GPT-3.5

- Improved text generation quality
- Enhanced prompt engineering
- Reduced model bias
- Incremental performance improvements

### GPT-4.0

1.7 trillion parameters, far beyond the number of parameters outline previously for GPT-3 models.

Human experts are mandatory for

1. Morals and ethics
2. Prompts and responses
3. Quality control and understanding the model

### Journal

Graffiti - extracts iXBRL and passes to LLM via api and question.

Need to give system prompts on what to do and what not to do.

Modern LLM have access to internet search and api/tools.

- GPT3 is a foundation model
- Foundation models can do many tasks it wasn't not trained for

Recommended reading

[Marr, B. (2021) Business trends in Practice, Wiley (Chapter 16 & 17)](https://www.vlebooks.com/Product/Index/2705166) – available on VLeBooks

Rothman, D. (2024) Transformers for Natural Language Processing and Computer Vision, Packt, Third Edition (Chapter 7) – available via Kortext

It is strongly advised that you read the recommended module reading list to ensure you have covered the learning outcomes fully. However, it is not compulsory that you read everything.

Bibliography and references

[Brown, T., Mann, B., Ryder, N., Subbiah, M., Kaplan, J.D., Dhariwal, P., Neelakantan, A., Shyam, P., Sastry, G., Askell, A. and Agarwal, S., (2020). Language models are few-shot learners. Advances in neural information processing systems, 33, pp.1877-1901. | arxiv.org](https://arxiv.org/abs/2005.14165)

[Radford, A., Wu, J., Child, R., Luan, D., Amodei, D. and Sutskever, I., (2019). Language models are unsupervised multitask learners. OpenAI blog, 1(8), p.9. | OpenAI](https://cdn.openai.com/better-language-models/language_models_are_unsupervised_multitask_learners.pdf)

[Rothman, D. (2024) Transformers for Natural Language Processing, Packt | VLeBooks |](https://www.vlebooks.com/Product/Index/2182859)

[Vaswani, A., Shazeer, N., Parmar, N., Uszkoreit, J., Jones, L., Gomez, A.N., Kaiser, Ł. and Polosukhin, I.,(2017) Attention is all you need. Advances in neural information processing systems, 30. | arxiv.org](https://arxiv.org/abs/1706.03762)

[Oh, Namkee & Choi, Gyu-Seong & Lee, Woo. (2023). ChatGPT goes to Operating Room: Evaluating GPT-4 Performance and the Future Direction of Surgical Education and Training in the Era of Large Language Models. | MedRxiv.org](https://www.medrxiv.org/content/10.1101/2023.03.16.23287340v1)
